Dick Sindicich, 323.930.5690 of Zhong Lun Law Firm appearing via Zoom for Cross-defendants Full Bright Properties, LLC., Z & L Properties Inc. and 1554 Market St. Development, LLC.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TRUMARK URBAN PARTNERS I, L.P., TRUMARK HOMES LLC Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order - 1554 Market Street Development is DENIED without prejudice. Burden is not met under CCP 483.010 (a), the only breach remaining at issue on the cross-complaint is failure to defend and the moving party seeks to attach costs/fees, which are also not fixed/ascertainable based on the contract. Great American Ins. Co. v. National Health Services, Inc. (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 785 is factually and legally distinguishable. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Leodis Matthews, Esq., 323-930-5690, appearing for FPC Builders, Inc., Cross-Defendants
Paymon Hifai, Esq., 925-943-6570, appearing for Cross-Complainant, Trumark Urban Partners

ARGUED. The Court reported that the tentative ruling stated the opposing party was supposed to file a declaration regarding fees. Mr. Hifai stated that he was unable to get to his office after the tentative was released. The Court responded that it did not receive a declaration or a request for an extension of time to prepare a declaration. Mr. Hifai orally stated the amount he is seeking. The Court stated that Mr Hifai's motion for fees and costs are denied for failure to follow the Court's tentative ruling. The court addressed the moving party and stated that it will allow one last chance.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
